location.denied: Location permission denied. Showing London data.

moderate.label: Moderate
moderate.advice: Sensitive individuals should wear a mask 😷

bad.label: Poor
bad.advice: Wear a mask when going outside ⚠️

hero.moderate: Sensitive individuals should wear a mask 😷
hero.high: Wear a mask when going outside ⚠️

decision.intro: Before you head out, check the one action that matters most today based on pollen and air quality.
decision.title: Most important action today
decision.pendingTitle: We’ll suggest today’s action after we confirm your location
decision.pendingDesc: We’re preparing a local forecast while your location is loading.

headline.take-medicine-early: Take your allergy medicine early today
headline.avoid-lunch-walk: Skip the lunch walk today if you can
headline.okay-today: You can probably follow your normal routine today

summary.take-medicine-early: Pollen pressure looks strong enough that it is better to prepare before symptoms ramp up.
summary.wear-mask: Air quality or mixed triggers look rough enough that reducing exposure early is the safer move.
summary.avoid-lunch-walk: Cutting outdoor time today could keep your afternoon symptoms more stable.
summary.okay-today: Today does not look especially aggressive. A normal routine should be fine if symptoms stay mild.

state.locationDenied: Location access was denied, so this recommendation uses a default city.
state.stale: Live data was delayed, so this recommendation is based on a recent cached forecast.
state.pollenOnly: This recommendation is based mainly on pollen data.
state.dustOnly: This recommendation is based mainly on air quality data.

actionList.take-medicine-early: Prepare your medicine before you head out
actionList.wear-mask: Start the commute with a mask
actionList.avoid-lunch-walk: Keep the lunch walk short or skip it

decision.detailHint: Raw numbers are supporting context. The decision above should come first.
decision.feedbackDesc: The fastest way to improve tomorrow’s recommendation is to record today’s symptoms.

diary.title: How are you feeling today?
diary.description: Track daily to discover your allergy patterns
diary.expandCta: Record symptoms and get personalized advice

pollenAlert.title: Pollen Alert
pollenAlert.desc: Pollen levels are high today. Combined with your symptoms, take extra precautions.

dustAlert.title: Air Quality Alert
dustAlert.desc: Air quality is poor today. Minimize outdoor time and wear a protective mask.

mask.title: Wear a mask
mask.desc: Use an FFP2/N95 mask outdoors. Regular masks don't filter pollen effectively.

stayIndoor.title: Stay indoors
stayIndoor.desc: When symptoms are severe, stay inside. Go out in the evening when pollen counts drop.

medicine.title: Consider antihistamines
medicine.desc: Over-the-counter antihistamines can help. Take them 30 minutes before going out.

eyeDrops.title: Use eye drops
eyeDrops.desc: Preservative-free artificial tears help flush pollen. Avoid rubbing your eyes.

sunglasses.title: Wear sunglasses
sunglasses.desc: Sunglasses reduce direct pollen contact with your eyes.

nasalRinse.title: Nasal rinse
nasalRinse.desc: Rinse with saline solution to physically remove pollen. 1-2 times daily.

humidifier.title: Use a humidifier
humidifier.desc: Keep indoor humidity at 40-50% to ease congestion.

warmDrink.title: Warm drinks
warmDrink.desc: Warm water or honey tea soothes throat irritation.

shower.title: Shower after going out
shower.desc: Shower and change clothes immediately after coming home. Pollen clings to skin and fabric.

sleep.title: Get enough sleep
sleep.desc: Allergies disrupt sleep quality. An antihistamine before bed may help.

ventilation.title: Time your ventilation
ventilation.desc: Ventilate during evening hours. Pollen counts peak between 6-10 AM.

clothes.title: Manage outdoor clothes
clothes.desc: Wash outdoor clothes right away or keep them by the entrance.

metadata.title: Achoo — Pollen & Air Quality Forecast
metadata.description: Before you head out, check whether to take medicine early, wear a mask, or reduce exposure. Real-time location-based allergy action guide.
